## Purpose
This file exists to configure the TypeScript compiler options for the project, specifying how TypeScript code should be compiled.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will observe that TypeScript files in the `src` directory are compiled to JavaScript in the `dist` directory, adhering to the specified compiler options.

## Business Rules
The configuration enforces strict type-checking, allows for ES2022 features, and ensures consistent casing in file names. It also specifies that JSON modules can be imported.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Input includes TypeScript files located in the `src` directory. Output consists of compiled JavaScript files generated in the `dist` directory.

## Side Effects
The configuration may lead to the creation of compiled JavaScript files in the specified output directory, affecting the project's build artifacts.

## Error And Edge Cases
If TypeScript files contain errors, the compilation process may fail, preventing output generation. Additionally, if the `src` directory is empty or incorrectly specified, no files will be compiled.

## Dependencies
The configuration specifies a dependency on Node.js types for type definitions, indicating that the project may utilize Node.js features.

## Operational Notes
The configuration allows for interoperation with ES modules and skips type checking for library files, which can improve compilation speed. It is important to maintain the directory structure as specified to ensure proper compilation.
